enonce.texte: Read the list below. Identify which items are RIGHTS of a child and which are DUTIES. Write 'R' for Right and 'D' for Duty.

questions.texte: 1. To go to school
2. To help with housework
3. To have good food to eat
4. To respect parents and teachers
5. To be protected from harm
6. To attend school regularly

intitule: Understanding Rights and Duties with 'Must' and 'Have the Right to'

enonce.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct phrase: 'must' (for duties) or 'has the right to' (for rights).

questions.texte: Every child ______ go to school.
A child ______ obey the rules of the school.
Every child ______ be protected from violence.
Children ______ respect their parents and elders.
A child ______ have enough food and clean water.
Children ______ help keep the environment clean.

enonce.texte: Read each situation and decide whether it describes a right or a duty. Then, explain your choice in one sentence.

questions.texte: A young girl receives a vaccination to stay healthy.
questions.reponse: This is a right because every child has the right to healthcare and protection from diseases.

questions.texte: A student cleans the classroom after the lesson.
questions.reponse: This is a duty because children must help keep their school clean and take responsibility for their environment.

questions.texte: A boy is allowed to go to school even though he is from a poor family.
questions.reponse: This is a right because every child has the right to education regardless of their background.

questions.texte: A student listens carefully to the teacher and does not disturb the class.
questions.reponse: This is a duty because children must respect their teachers and follow classroom rules.

intitule: Asking and Answering About Rights and Duties

enonce.texte: Imagine you are interviewing a classmate about children's rights and duties. Match the questions on the left with the correct answers on the right.

questions.texte: Question: What are the rights of a child?
questions.reponse: Answer: Every child has the right to education, health, protection, food, and shelter.

questions.texte: Question: What must a child do at school?
questions.reponse: Answer: A child must attend school regularly and obey the school rules.

questions.texte: Question: Why is it important to respect parents?
questions.reponse: Answer: It is important because parents take care of us and help us grow.

enonce.texte: Write 3-4 sentences to describe one right and one duty of a child. Use the verbs 'must' or 'have the right to'.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph here.
questions.reponse: Every child has the right to education. This means they must go to school to learn. Children also have duties: they must respect their teachers and help at home. These duties help them become good citizens.

intitule: Role-Play: Asking About Rights and Duties

enonce.texte:
Work with a partner. One student is a reporter and the other is a child. The reporter asks the child questions about their rights and duties. Use the phrases below to help you.

Reporter: What are the rights of a child? / What must a child do? / Why is it important to respect parents?

Child: Every child has the right to... / A child must... / It is important because...

questions.texte: Write a short dialogue (5-6 exchanges) between the reporter and the child.
questions.reponse:
Reporter: Hello! What are the rights of a child?
Child: Every child has the right to education and health.
Reporter: What must a child do?
Child: A child must respect parents and obey school rules.
Reporter: Why is it important to respect parents?
Child: It is important because parents care for us and teach us many things.

enonce.texte: Read the two situations below. For each one, identify one right and one duty that are involved. Then, explain why this is important.

questions.texte: Situation 1: A school provides free lunches for all students. Identify the right and the duty.
questions.reponse: Right: The right to food and nutrition. Duty: Students must attend school regularly and participate in classes to benefit from this right.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why this right is important.
sousQuestions.reponse: This right is important because it ensures that children have enough energy to learn and stay healthy, which helps them succeed in school.

questions.texte: Situation 2: A community organizes a clean-up campaign at the local health center. Identify the right and the duty.
questions.reponse: Right: The right to a clean and healthy environment. Duty: Community members, including children, must help keep the environment clean.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why this duty is important.
sousQuestions.reponse: This duty is important because a clean environment prevents diseases and protects everyone's health, especially children who are more vulnerable.

intitule: Creating a Poster on Children's Rights

enonce.texte: Create a simple poster (in words) that lists two rights and two duties of a child. Use present forms of regular verbs and include an illustration idea.

questions.texte: Write the text for your poster here.
questions.reponse: Children's Rights: 1. Every child has the right to education. 2. Every child has the right to be protected from harm. Children's Duties: 1. Every child must obey school rules. 2. Every child must respect their parents and elders. Illustration idea: Draw a picture of children in a classroom and children helping at home.

intitule: Complete the Sentences About Rights and Duties

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct words from the box.

box: right, duty, education, respect, protection, obey

questions.texte: 1. Every child has the ______ to go to school.
2. It is a ______ to help with household chores.
3. All children need ______ from violence.
4. Children must ______ their parents and teachers.
5. It is a right to receive ______.
6. Children must ______ the rules of the community.

enonce.texte: Read each statement and decide if it is a right, a duty, or both. Justify your answer in 1-2 sentences.

questions.texte: A child receives free medical care.
questions.reponse: This is a right because every child has the right to healthcare. It is also a duty of the government to provide it.

questions.texte: A student helps a younger sibling with homework.
questions.reponse: This is a duty because children must support their family and help others.

questions.texte: A child participates in a community meeting to discuss safety.
questions.reponse: This is both a right and a duty. It is a right to have a voice in matters that affect them, and a duty to participate in community life.
